heading: Determination of eligibility and project selection
label: 3907 Determination of eligibility and project selection

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The project and obligor shall be creditworthy, which shall be determined by the Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: the terms, conditions, financial structure, and security features of the proposed financing;

children.label: ii
children.text: the dedicated revenue sources that will secure or fund the project obligations;

children.label: iii
children.text: the financial assumptions upon which the project is based; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the financial soundness and credit history of the obligor.

children.heading: Security features
children.label: C Security features
children.text: The Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable, shall ensure that any financing for the project has appropriate security features, such as a rate covenant, supporting the project obligations to ensure repayment.

children.heading: Preliminary rating opinion letter
children.label: i Preliminary rating opinion letter
children.text: The Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable, shall require each project applicant to provide, at the time of application, a preliminary rating opinion letter from at least 1 rating agency indicating that the senior obligations of the project (which may be the Federal credit instrument) have the potential to achieve an investment-grade rating.

children.heading: Final rating opinion letters
children.label: ii Final rating opinion letters
children.text: The Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable, shall require each project applicant to provide, prior to final acceptance and financing of the project, a final rating opinion letter from at least 1 rating agency indicating that the senior obligations of the project have an investment-grade rating.

children.heading: Special rule for certain combined projects
children.label: E Special rule for certain combined projects
i
children.text: a State infrastructure financing authority for a project under , which may include requiring the provision of a final rating opinion letter from at least one rating agency; orsection 3905(9) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an entity for a project under , which may include requiring the provision of a final rating opinion letter from at least two rating agencies.section 3905(10) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the eligible project costs of a project shall be reasonably anticipated to be not less than $20,000,000.

children.heading: Small community water infrastructure projects
children.label: B Small community water infrastructure projects
children.text: For a project described in paragraph (2) or (3) of that serves a community of not more than 25,000 individuals, the eligible project costs of a project shall be reasonably anticipated to be not less than $5,000,000.section 3905 of this title

children.heading: Dedicated revenue sources
children.label: 3 Dedicated revenue sources
children.text: The Federal credit instrument for the project shall be repayable, in whole or in part, from dedicated revenue sources that also secure the project obligations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If an eligible project is carried out by an entity that is not a State or local government or an agency or instrumentality of a State or local government or a tribal government or consortium of tribal governments, the project shall be publicly sponsored.

children.heading: Public sponsorship
children.label: B Public sponsorship
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, a project shall be considered to be publicly sponsored if the obligor can demonstrate, to the satisfaction of the Secretary or the Administrator, as appropriate, that the project applicant has consulted with the affected State, local, or tribal government in which the project is located, or is otherwise affected by the project, and that such government supports the proposed project.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: A Notification
children.text: For each eligible project for which the Administrator has authority under paragraph (2) or (3) of and for which the Administrator has received an application for financial assistance under this chapter, the Administrator shall notify, not later than 30 days after the date on which the Administrator receives a complete application, the applicable State infrastructure financing authority of the State in which the project is located that such application has been submitted.section 3902(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: by the date that is 180 days after the date of receipt of a notification under subparagraph (A), the State infrastructure financing authority fails to enter into an assistance agreement to provide funds for the project; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the financial assistance to be provided by the State infrastructure financing authority will be at rates and terms that are less favorable than the rates and terms for financial assistance provided under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable, shall determine whether an applicant for assistance under this chapter has developed, and identified adequate revenues to implement, a plan for operating, maintaining, and repairing the project over the useful life of the project.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: An eligible project described in that has not been specifically authorized by Congress shall not be eligible for Federal assistance for operations and maintenance.section 3905(1) of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary or the Administrator, as applicable, shall establish criteria for the selection of projects that meet the eligibility requirements of subsection (a), in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: the reduction of flood risk;

children.label: ii
children.text: the improvement of water quality and quantity, including aquifer recharge;

children.label: iii
children.text: the protection of drinking water, including source water protection; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the support of international commerce.

children.label: B
children.text: The extent to which the project financing plan includes public or private financing in addition to assistance under this chapter.

children.label: C
children.text: The likelihood that assistance under this chapter would enable the project to proceed at an earlier date than the project would otherwise be able to proceed.

children.label: D
children.text: The extent to which the project uses new or innovative approaches.

children.label: E
children.text: The amount of budget authority required to fund the Federal credit instrument made available under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: protects against extreme weather events, such as floods or hurricanes; or

children.label: ii
children.text: helps maintain or protect the environment.

children.label: G
children.text: The extent to which a project serves regions with significant energy exploration, development, or production areas.

children.label: i
children.text: water quality concerns in areas of regional, national, or international significance;

children.label: ii
children.text: water quantity concerns related to groundwater, surface water, or other water sources;

children.label: iii
children.text: significant flood risk;

children.label: iv
children.text: water resource challenges identified in existing regional, State, or multistate agreements; or

children.label: v
children.text: water resources with exceptional recreational value or ecological importance.

children.label: I
children.text: The extent to which the project addresses identified municipal, State, or regional priorities.

children.label: J
children.text: The readiness of the project to proceed toward development, including a demonstration by the obligor that there is a reasonable expectation that the contracting process for construction of the project can commence by not later than 90 days after the date on which a Federal credit instrument is obligated for the project under this chapter.

children.label: K
children.text: The extent to which assistance under this chapter reduces the contribution of Federal assistance to the project.

children.heading: Special rule for certain combined projects
children.label: 3 Special rule for certain combined projects
children.text: For a project described in , the Administrator shall only consider the criteria described in subparagraphs (B) through (K) of paragraph (2).section 3905(9) of this title

children.heading: Federal requirements
children.label: c Federal requirements
children.text: Nothing in this section supersedes the applicability of other requirements of Federal law (including regulations).
